title: Playwright-driven UI exploration (model-based testing)
manager: Point an FSL machine at a real web UI and let it drive: the machine enumerates legal event sequences, Playwright executes them against the live page, and any divergence between what the machine expected and what the UI actually did is a bug in one of the two. This is model-based testing with the machine as oracle, aimed at the messiest target there is — a running browser. Instead of hand-writing hundreds of brittle click-scripts, you describe the app as a state machine once and get an exploration engine that finds the reachable-but-unhandled corners nobody thinks to script. It turns the era's 'machine-as-oracle' idea into a concrete, run-it-today testing tool for front-end applications.
cs: the MBT harness (W8.11) specialized to a Playwright driver; walk strategies (random/coverage-guided) generate event sequences; an adapter maps machine events to DOM actions and machine states to page assertions; a divergence is a failing tape, shrinkable to a minimal reproduction.
